title: Slightly Confused About SEELE Gendo And NERVs Plans
display_title: Slightly Confused About SEELE Gendo And NERVs Plans
wikitext:
[]: Slightly Confused About SEELE, Gendo And NERV's Plans...             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Fri Feb 22, 2013 11:13 am OK, so, I understand most of what's going on in Evangelion pretty well. However, I have become a bit confused as to what is actually going on. My understanding is that if an Angel reaches Adam, then humanity will be wiped out. If Adam and Lilith are merged, then Instrumentality will initiate, and everything on Earth, Angels, humans, Adam and Lilith, will become one immeasurably powerful entity, at once everyone and no-one. To make sure this never happened, each Seed of Life has a Lance of Longinus in it, a weapon that will make sure that if 2 Seeds of Life land on the same planet, only 1 will remain active, with the other Seed going dormant, as the "active" Seed's Lance will be destroyed. When humanity discovered Adam, one of them accidentally touched him, fusing with him and awakening him, causing 2nd Impact and awakening the Angels. SEELE is trying to initiate Instrumentality, if I remember rightly. Now, to do this, Adam must merge with Lilith. So where exactly does the Lance of Longuinus and the Evangelion Units come into it? And why don't SEELE just merge Adam and Lilith to start with, rather than waiting for all the Angels to be destroyed? Also, if I remember correctly, Gendo is trying to use Rei and Adam to intiate a mini-Instrumentality, which will reunite him with his wife. In End of Evangelion in the 3rd Impact sequence, Gendo is killed by Yui Ikari, in the guise of Unit 01, cutting him out of Instrumentality altogether. Except...Unit 01 is currently entombed within the Tree of Life, which is in orbit...and what is the Tree of Life, and what does it have to do with Instrumentality? Is the Unit 01 that devours Gendo actually an extent of Yui's will, appearing in a similar way to the Reis that tang everybody in NERV, except destroying him instead of tanging him? NERV, at the very least, has a sensible goal, destroying the Angels (it just so happens it's being manipulated by SEELE and Gendo). And then Rebuild 2.22 gets even MORE confusing, with Unit 01 starting 3rd Impact when it goes completely berserk and semi-merges with Zeruel when Shinji attempts to save Rei from the core of the Angel, despite neither the Lance of Longuinus nor Adam nor Lilith being involved and ARGH... I'm not pulling an "OMG TEH STORY SUX ITS SO CONFUSING!!1!!111!ONE!!1", I genuinely don't understand what's going on with Instrumentality... Can someone please put me out of my misery and explain?

[]:             Quote Post by Atropos » Fri Feb 22, 2013 11:21 am The Angels have to be destroyed first so that they don't take over the world after everyone gets tanged. There's also the prophecy to consider; Seele takes that seriously. And no one knows what the Tree of Life is, so don't even ask.

[]:             Quote Post by Merkaba » Fri Feb 22, 2013 11:29 am The Tree of Life is what would give the new, consonant being infinite life.

[]:             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Fri Feb 22, 2013 11:35 am Ah, OK, I get it. So Adam needs to be merged with Lilith to start 3rd Impact, and the Lance becomes the Tree of Life to act as a vessel for the collective soul of humanity, Adam and Lilith. And SEELE, being a bunch of superstitious bastards, want to follow the Dead Sea Scrolls to the letter. So, I assume the Evangelions were used to lend their power to the anti-AT field, and to form the Tree of Life, with Unit 01 forming the centre, as it is made from Lilith. So that's that sort of explained. So, what about the Rebuild 3rd Impact (which was narrowly averted by Kaworu)? And I thought that Kaworu was working for SEELE, who were trying to CAUSE 3rd Impact? Or is that a different kind of 3rd Impact to the one from End of Evangelion?

[]:             Quote Post by Merkaba » Fri Feb 22, 2013 11:43 am It seems to be similar to NGE's Impact. However, recall that, even though Kaworu works for Seele, they still hadn't fulfilled the covenant with Lilith which was necessary to carry out a proper Instrumentality. So maybe it's BECAUSE he worked for Seele that he stopped Shinji, since that's not the route they wanted to take. Again, it's like in NGE, where they have (or want) to follow the prophecies from the Secret Dead Sea Scrolls.

[]:             Quote Post by user-02 » Fri Feb 22, 2013 12:18 pm This is a decent guide, and I find myself consulting it fairly often when I am confused: http://wiki.evageeks.org/Third_Impact (Be warned: very slight Rebuild 2.0/3.0-interim spoilers at the bottom of the page)

[]:             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Fri Feb 22, 2013 12:29 pm Thanks, User 02. That really helps!
